reason: Response B uses more distinctive phrasing and structural choices that feel characteristic of my writing style — particularly the "the part nobody photographs" metaphor, the phrase "living inside the chapter that usually gets edited out," and the specific cadence of "a little awkwardly, a little stubbornly." Response A is competent and warm but leans on more familiar, almost generic encouragement tropes ("raw talent," "keep showing up") that feel closer to a generic imitation. The closing line of B ("frustrated and trying anyway. That combination is rarer than talent, and it lasts longer") has a compression and rhythm I recognize as more natural to my outputs.
